g.text: - The context is used to restart a goroutine after pausing
- Includes the low and high address for the stack range
- G is on a run queue
- G is attached to an M
sched -> Buffer used to save goroutine "context"
(pointer to head of M linked list)
gFree -> Global cache of _Gdead goroutines to be reused
pidle -> List of all idle P's (_Pidle)
midle -> List of all idle/sleeping M's waiting for work
stack -> The address bounds of the stack
m -> Pointer to the M executing this goroutine G
g0 -> Pointer to G used to run system code (ex. Scheduling)
curg -> Pointer to the User Goroutine running on this thread
p -> Pointer to P attached to this M
- G is blocked by a Go mechanism
- Ex. waiting on a channel send
- G may be put on a "free list" for re-use
m -> Poiner to M this P is attached to
gFree -> Local list of _Gdead G structs to be reused
runq -> Local run queue of G's
- G's are in _Grunnable state, queued to be executed
runnext -> Single G that should be run next
- This slot is used for fast back and forth channel communication
- P is owned by an M
- P is paused for garbage collection
Pointer to M this P is
